title: Hermes Skill Guard Rule File
description: Declarative rule definitions consumed by the configurable rule engine (T10). A rule file is loaded by hermes-skill-guard and merged with the built-in default rules.

version.description: Schema version of the rule file. Currently only '1.0' is accepted.

disabled_rules.description: Built-in or previously loaded rule ids to disable. Useful for opting out of a default rule without forking the codebase.

rules.description: List of rule definitions. Evaluated in array order; the first matching rule of each severity contributes its reason to the decision.

id.description: Stable, dotted rule identifier. Must be unique within the merged rule set (default + user).

description.description: Human-readable summary of what the rule checks and why it exists.

enabled.description: When false, the rule is loaded but skipped during evaluation. Default: true.

priority.description: Lower numbers are evaluated first within the same severity bucket. Default: 100.

when.description: Boolean condition tree. The rule fires when the tree evaluates to true against the current ToolCall context.

severity.description: Severity contributed to the final decision. The engine picks the highest severity across firing rules: info < warn < candidate < block. 'info' rules add a reason but never change the decision from allow.

message.description: Reason text appended to Decision.reasons. May reference fields via {field_name} placeholders (e.g. {skill_name}). Unknown placeholders are left untouched.

condition.description: Either a leaf condition (op + field/value) or a logical combinator (and / or / not).

field.description: Context field name. Available: skill_name, tool_name, content, content_length, description, target_path, dry_run, enforcement_mode.

value.description: Comparison value. Required for all ops except 'missing' and 'present'. Type depends on op: string for equals/contains/matches; integer for length_*; boolean for equals on boolean fields.

ignore_case.description: Apply case-insensitive comparison for string ops. Default: false.
